reasoning: By late October 2025, 9 months of temperature data are already available. The market price for this event dropped to 2% (0.02) on October 16, 2025. Based on the climate cycle, 2023 and 2024 were supercharged by an El Niño event making them record-breakingly hot, while 2025 saw a transition into a La Niña pattern, which typically lowers global average temperatures relative to El Niño years. The extremely low market estimate strongly implies that the year-to-date data for 2025 is lagging significantly behind the 2024 record, making it mathematically nearly impossible for 2025 to catch up in the remaining months. Therefore, I am assigning the minimum allowable probability of 0.05.
